mission.title: kured: Request: images for other architectures
mission.description: Request: images for other architectures. Requested by 13+ users.

steps.title: Check current kured deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kured version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kured -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kured
helm list -n kured 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kured installation.

steps.title: Review kured configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kured configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kured -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kured
kubectl get configmap -n kured -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kured
```
Feature request: Please provide images for other architectures (more than amd64) as part of your release.

kured needs to run on every node, and for my cluster that means amd64, arm, and arm64.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Request: images for other architectures
steps.description:
This is a WIP-PR of a build with ARM support.
I'm not really sure about the changes in the Makefile. With the changes for windows-support in mind from #460 this would create more and more complicated tooling-scripts to maintain.

My personal favourite is to do only the go-compilation and other small tasks in the makefile and move the image build and -publish to Github Actions which are already able to create multiarch-builds out of the box. But this would remove the ability to create images

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kured -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kured
kubectl get events -n kured --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Request: images for other architectures" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This is a WIP-PR of a build with ARM support.
I'm not really sure about the changes in the Makefile. With the changes for windows-support in mind from #460 this would create more and more complicated tooling-scripts to maintain.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kured installed or the issue environment reproducible.
